name: Utrecht (Netherlands)

colors.symbolism: from the arms of the historic Bishopric of Utrecht
from the arms of the historic Bishopric of Utrecht

description: Horizontally divided white over red, with a red canton in the upper hoist charged with a white cross.
history: Adopted by the provincial states on 15 January 1952. The flag combines the per-pale red-and-white arms of the bishopric (rendered as horizontal halves) with the historic banner of the City of Utrecht in canton.

features.symbolism: the red and white derive from the arms of the medieval Bishopric of Utrecht (per pale gules and argent), the territorial predecessor of the modern province
the red canton with a white cross is the historic banner of the city and bishopric of Utrecht
